paper.title: Diverse Chemo-Dynamical Properties of Nitrogen-Rich Stars Identified From Low-Resolution Spectra

extraction.summary: This paper uses low-resolution spectroscopy (SEGUE/SEGUE-2, LAMOST) to identify nitrogen-rich (N-rich) stars in the Galactic halo, which are believed to be remnants of globular cluster (GC) second-generation stars tidally stripped from their parent clusters. The study examines the chemo-dynamical properties of these stars (orbital parameters rmax, rmin, zmax, eccentricity, etc.), divides them into multiple subgroups (MINR, MINT, MINT-E, MAXR, etc.), and discusses their possible origins (GSE, Sgr, Sequoia dwarf galaxies, etc.). All stars are gravitationally bound to the Milky Way; the paper never discusses Galactic escape velocity, hypervelocity stars (HVS), or the possibility of stars leaving the Galaxy. No HVS candidates.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The 26 nitrogen-rich (N-rich) star sample from Table 1. Identified via SEGUE/SEGUE-2 and LAMOST low-resolution spectroscopy, combined with Gaia DR2/EDR3 proper motions and Bailer-Jones distances, orbital parameters (maximum/minimum Galactocentric distance, maximum disk distance, eccentricity, etc.) were computed using galpy.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This paper studies nitrogen-rich stars that escaped from globular clusters in the Galactic halo. Although these stars move through the halo, they are all gravitationally bound to the Milky Way — the orbital parameters calculated in the paper (rmax, rmin, zmax) all lie within the range of bound Galactic orbits, with eccentricity e < 1. The paper never discusses Galactic escape velocity, hypervelocity stars (HVS), or gravitational-unbound status. The research focuses on the dynamical property classification of chemically peculiar stars (N-rich) and is completely unrelated to HVS.

source_refs.context: Section 2.3: 计算轨道参数（rmax, rmin, zmax, eccentricity），所有恒星轨道均在银河系束缚范围内。

Section 4.1: 'a small fraction of our MINR stars have orbits that pass close to the Galactic center (rmin < 3 kpc), but are not bound to the bulge.' 这里 'not bound to the bulge' 指不束缚于核球（即非核球星），并非指脱离银河系引力束缚。
Summary: 讨论 N-rich 恒星可能来自 GSE、Sgr、Sequoia 等矮星系，均为银河系内束缚轨道上的恒星，从未提及 HVS 或银河系逃逸。
